rationale: The record contains several scoped evaluation results supporting the bounded comparison, with material protocol limitations.
The narrow claim of competitiveness on at least one workflow is more likely than not, but deployment relevance remains uncertain.
Reported gains exist, but the record lacks the independent matched replication required by the proposition.
The evidence is too scoped and mixed to make broad capability superiority more likely than not.
A limited retrieval result does not make cross-task reliable long-context use more likely than not.
The cited controlled findings strongly support the methodological distinction between capacity and reliable use.
Selected matched task reports support the bounded monetary-cost comparison.
The record omits enough latency, reliability, hardware, and correction-cost information that lower total cost is less likely than not established.
The deployment and governance differences between hosted access and downloadable weights are directly material.
The record identifies operational burdens that token price alone does not represent.
Multiple available reports identify the stated failure modes as live risks.
No deployment-specific control-effectiveness record establishes that risks fall within institutional tolerance.
Unresolved control effectiveness and operational risks make suitability for minimally supervised long-horizon work less likely than not.
The bounded research record does not contain a public independent matched replication.
The selected evaluations show mixed scoped results rather than broad superiority.
The run-date evidence indicates checkpoint release and final license terms were not yet verified.
